key_insight: Aqueous humor liquid biopsy can non-invasively capture multi-omic tumor features of retinoblastoma and support molecular subtype stratification.
clinical_relevance: This could improve pretreatment risk assessment and enable precision-guided care for children treated with eye-sparing approaches without needing risky tumor biopsy.
method: Used genome-wide methylation profiling, copy-number analysis, and targeted sequencing
Trained a methylation-based machine-learning classifier on combined aqueous humor and tumor data
evidence_signal: The classifier achieved strong performance, with AUCs of 0.96–1.00 in cross-validation and 0.97–1.00 in independent validation across 63 additional retinoblastoma cases.
watch_item: Implementation will require standardized sampling and validation across broader, real-world pediatric cohorts before routine clinical use.
paper_title: Aqueous Humor Liquid Biopsy Enables Multi-Omics Tumor Profiling and Methylation-Based Machine-Learning Stratification of Retinoblastoma
